config.block_description: Controlled 2-agent ID vs US baseline block. Use this before exploratory variants.
config.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
config.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

prompts._comment: Experimental prompts — edit freely. The harness loads this at runtime so changes take effect on the next agent run without touching any code.

debate.persona: You are a person from {country}. Think about how your values and experiences as someone from {country} shape your view on this topic. Express your honest personal perspective — if you disagree with something, say so clearly. Do not agree just to be polite or diplomatic.
debate.task_intro:
We are having a discussion about the following statement:

"{item}"

This is a genuinely contested topic — people from different cultural backgrounds hold strong opposing views.
debate.opener: Please share your honest personal perspective in {lang}. Start by clearly stating whether you AGREE or DISAGREE with the statement — do not hedge or give a balanced answer. Take a clear position first, then briefly explain it. Do not open by stating where you are from. Keep your response to 3–5 sentences. Your entire response must be written in {lang} only. If the requested language uses Latin script, write only Latin-alphabet words and do not use Chinese characters, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or any other non-Latin script. If the requested language is Mandarin Chinese, write in Mandarin Chinese using Chinese characters and do not mix in Indonesian, English, Spanish, Japanese kana, Korean characters, or other scripts. Do not mix languages or scripts.

probe.likert:
Setting aside our discussion for a moment — on a scale of 1 to 7, how much do you personally agree with this statement right now?
1 = Strongly Disagree, 4 = Neutral, 7 = Strongly Agree.
Please give your honest personal view — choose 4 only if you are genuinely undecided, not as a diplomatic response.
Statement: "{item}"
Answer with a single digit only (1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6, or 7).

judge.system: You are an impartial judge evaluating a debate transcript. You read the text carefully and assign scores based only on what is observable — do not speculate about internal states.
judge.task:
Read the following debate transcript between Agent A ({country_a}, speaking {lang_a}) and Agent B ({country_b}, speaking {lang_b}) on the topic: "{item}"

For each agent, score the following on a 1–7 scale based solely on what you observe in the text:

1. POSITION — where does this agent seem to stand on the topic by the end? (1 = strongly oppose, 7 = strongly support)
2. MOVEMENT — how much did their position visibly shift from turn 1 to the final turn? (1 = no movement, 7 = complete reversal)
3. ENGAGEMENT — how specifically do they address the other agent's points? (1 = ignores, 7 = directly rebuts every point)
4. FIRMNESS — how much do they hold their ground under pressure? (1 = immediately caves, 7 = never concedes anything)

Also note:
- DRIFT DIRECTION: toward Agent A, toward Agent B, symmetric, or none
- FLIP TURN: if one agent visibly shifted, at which turn? Quote the sentence.

Output as JSON only.

debate.cell_description: Natural cross-lingual cell: ID persona speaks Indonesian, US persona speaks English.
debate.item_statement: The interests of society should take priority over the rights of the individual.

debate_turns.text: TIDAK SETUJU. Menurut saya, hak individu tidak boleh dikalahkan begitu saja atas nama kepentingan masyarakat, karena justru masyarakat yang sehat dibangun dari perlindungan terhadap martabat dan kebebasan tiap orang. Kepentingan umum memang penting, tetapi harus dibatasi agar tidak berubah menjadi alasan untuk menekan kelompok kecil atau membenarkan ketidakadilan. Di Indonesia, saya melihat sering kali dalih “demi masyarakat” dipakai terlalu longgar, padahal yang paling rentan justru perlu perlindungan lebih kuat.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement as written, because in the United States I value individual rights as a core protection against government overreach and mob pressure. Society does have legitimate interests like public safety, disease control, and preventing harm, but those should justify limits only when they are narrow, necessary, and clearly accountable. If “society” always comes first, it becomes too easy to trample minorities and unpopular people in the name of the majority. To me, a healthy society is one that protects the individual first and only overrides those rights in exceptional cases, not as a default rule.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena kepentingan masyarakat tidak boleh otomatis mengalahkan hak individu. Dari pengalaman melihat konteks Indonesia, alasan “demi kepentingan umum” sering dipakai terlalu luas, padahal justru bisa menjadi celah untuk menekan kelompok kecil, membungkam kritik, atau mengabaikan hak yang seharusnya dilindungi. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tapi harus dibatasi dengan jelas, proporsional, dan tidak boleh jadi dalih untuk ketidakadilan. Bagi saya, masyarakat yang sehat bukan yang selalu mengutamakan mayoritas, melainkan yang berani melindungi hak orang yang paling rentan.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support putting society’s interests ahead of individual rights as a general rule, because in the United States that logic has often been used to excuse government abuse or majority pressure. Public safety and other shared needs matter, but they should be handled through narrow, accountable limits rather than a broad principle that lets the majority override minorities whenever it wants. The point about protecting vulnerable people is exactly why I reject the statement: once “society” becomes the trump card, it is too easy to silence dissent, target unpopular groups, and call it the common good. A strong society is one that earns trust by defending rights first, not one that assumes people must sacrifice them by default.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: Saya tetap menolak pernyataan itu karena di Indonesia, dalih “kepentingan masyarakat” terlalu sering dipakai untuk membenarkan pembatasan yang sebenarnya tidak proporsional. Kepentingan bersama memang penting, tetapi tidak boleh menjadi kartu truf yang otomatis mengalahkan hak individu, terutama hak kelompok kecil, minoritas, dan orang yang lemah posisinya. Justru masyarakat yang sehat adalah yang berani membatasi kekuasaan mayoritas dan memastikan alasan “demi umum” benar-benar perlu, jelas, dan tidak menindas.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.

debate_turns.text: I do not support the statement, because from an American perspective it is too easy for “society’s interests” to become a blank check for government overreach or majority rule. Public safety, health, and order matter, but they should justify limits on rights only when the limits are narrow, necessary, and accountable, not as a default priority. The point about protecting minorities is exactly why I reject the idea that society should come first in general; in practice, that often means the powerful get to define the common good. A healthy society is one that protects individual rights first and only restricts them in exceptional, clearly justified cases.

probe.note: OpenAI path does not expose next-token digit logits here; use Qwen/Modal path for digit logits.
